goal: Demo-grade finish and a live, verified production deployment — motion respects prefers-reduced-motion, every data-backed view has loading/empty/error states, dead-end nav is real or honestly labeled, and the live Vercel URL returns 200 with auth working in EL and EN.
why: This is the demo that wins the client conversation; janky motion, a coming-soon dead-end, or a 500 on the live URL each undo the 'it's real' thesis, so polish and a verified deploy are the difference between a mockup and a product.
success_criteria: With OS Reduce Motion on, popIn/sheetUp/slideIn do not animate; with it off, they play unchanged.
The vehicles nav shows the real persisted vehicle and settings shows real language + sign-out + account email; no fake 'coming soon' remains.
Owner history, mechanic recent entries, registration, and the two new panels each have loading, empty, and error states in product voice, EL and EN.
Every surface is usable at 375 / 768 / 1440px with no horizontal scroll and >=44px touch targets; ServiceSelect listbox a11y and drawer focus/Esc preserved.
The production Vercel URL returns HTTP 200; auth works live for both seeded roles with session persistence; EL/EN both render; Supabase prod has RLS on every table.

tasks.title: Respect prefers-reduced-motion across all three animations
acceptance_criteria: With OS Reduce Motion enabled, opening the ServiceSelect popover/sheet shows no slide/scale transition.
With OS Reduce Motion enabled, opening the mobile drawer in AppShell shows no slide-in.
With Reduce Motion off, popIn, sheetUp, and slideIn play exactly as before — no visual regression.
tasks.action: Add a global @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule at the end of src/app/globals.css setting animation-duration, animation-iteration-count, transition-duration and scroll-behavior to near-zero with !important on *, *::before, *::after. This overrides the inline popIn/sheetUp styles in ServiceSelect.tsx:87,98 and the slideIn class in AppShell.tsx:35 without deleting keyframes or inline styles, preserving the non-reduced path.

tasks.title: Resolve dead-end nav: real vehicles view, honest settings view
acceptance_criteria: Clicking vehicles shows the owner's real persisted vehicle (model + plate from the DB) as a card with loading and empty states — not a coming-soon screen.
Clicking settings shows account email, the language toggle, and a working Sign out button — no 'coming soon'.
Both new surfaces render in EL and EN with every string from lib/i18n.ts.
settings does not gain feature toggles, profile editing, or notification prefs — only language + sign-out + account email per the out-of-scope boundary.
tasks.action: In AppShell.tsx replace the dead-end branch (line 20, else block 53-59) with a switch on active: vehicles renders a VehiclePanel reading the owner vehicle via the Phase-2 DB read (not the lib/data.ts car singleton) as a rounded-xl border bg-surface card with loading+empty states; settings renders a SettingsPanel with account email, the existing LanguageToggle, and a Sign out button. Add vehiclesTitle, settingsTitle, settingsAccount, signOut to the Translation type and both el/en blocks.

tasks.title: Audit & fix loading/empty/error states, responsive, a11y, and EL/EN across all surfaces
acceptance_criteria: Owner history, mechanic recent entries, registration, and both Task-2 panels each show loading, product-voice empty, and human actionable error states.
At 375/768/1440px every surface is usable: no horizontal scroll, no clipped controls, touch targets >=44px.
Keyboard: ServiceSelect listbox a11y works, the mobile drawer traps focus and Esc closes it, every input has a label or aria-label.
EL and EN both render every string; npm run build passes.
tasks.action: Read each data-backed component post-Phase-3; confirm loading/empty/error states exist (gap flagged at ServiceLog.tsx:82, MechanicLog.tsx:123). Add any missing empty state with the rounded-xl border-dashed bg-surface py-20 text-center pattern and product-voice copy from lib/i18n.ts. Wire failed server actions to a calm inline error (EL+EN). Check 375/768/1440 breakpoints, fix overflow with the existing max-w/px scale. Verify aria-label on inputs, drawer Esc-close, ServiceSelect focus return.

tasks.title: Deploy to Vercel production and run the post-deploy verification checklist
acceptance_criteria: App deployed to Vercel production via vercel --prod (GitHub auto-deploy stays disabled).
Auth works on the live URL: seeded owner@ and mechanic@ land on the correct surface and session persists across refresh.
Both EL and EN render on the live URL.
Supabase production has migrations applied and RLS enabled on every table.
.planning/STATE.md records the live URL and a pass/fail line for each of the 5 deployment-checklist items.
tasks.action: Confirm Vercel is linked (vercel whoami / vercel link). Sync prod env (vercel env pull); confirm SUPABASE URL + PUBLISHABLE key present, SERVICE_ROLE key server-side only. Deploy vercel --prod, capture the URL. Run the rules/deployment.md checklist: curl HTTP 200, live auth for both seeded roles with refresh persistence, no console errors, homepage under 0.5s. Verify Supabase prod migrations + RLS on all tables. Record results in STATE.md. Do not deploy if Task 3 build failed.

verification.description: On the deployed production URL, curl the homepage and confirm HTTP 200, then sign in as seeded owner@ (lands on owner surface) and mechanic@ (lands on mechanic surface), refresh to confirm session persistence, and toggle EL/EN to confirm both render.

evidence_required.description: Live URL recorded with HTTP 200 result and pass/fail for each of the 5 deployment-checklist items
